propositions: Migrate operation certificates from per-clone symmetric HMAC self-attestations to asymmetric Ed25519 signatures attributed to the signing environment and verifiable by any third party against that environment's public key.

A project can optionally require a certificate from a specific trusted environment for a gate, pinned out-of-band in .rebar/trusted_environments.yaml on the Gerrit-gated code branch (never the tickets branch), verifying against the pinned key rather than the self-claimed DSSE keyid.

Certificates bind {ticket id, material fingerprint, merged-log position as a main commit SHA} so they cannot be replayed onto another ticket or stale state, with environment-key rotation using explicit-SHA era-scoped validity reusing the commit-ancestry rule and key_not_valid_at_era verdict.

A trusted rebar-mcp remote-execution path fetches authoritative ticket state and code-at-commit itself and signs the gate result, so a client cannot forge a passing verdict, deployed as a library-mode HTTP service on ECS Fargate behind API Gateway SigV4 with the signing key in Secrets Manager.
The merge-gate CI job extends the shipped verify-identity.yaml merged-log walk to verify the required-environment certificate and fail tickets that must carry one but do not.

Remove the legacy HMAC scheme in a contract phase with an expand-contract migration artifact, leaving both operation-cert and authorship signatures asymmetric while the default local path stays offline and never wedges an operation.
